payload.query: In times of gridlock, presidents resort to unilateral powers. According to Howell, what constitutes a unilateral presidential power? What are the tradeoffs of acting alone versus working with Congress? Under what circumstances might a president accept these tradeoffs?

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court’s Decision on Trump’s Sweeping Tariffs
articles.abstract: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.
articles.snippet: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.

articles.title: The Legality, or Illegality, of Killing a Foreign Leader, Explained
articles.abstract: The United States and Israel started a war with Iran by killing its supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

articles.title: Fresh Off a Supreme Court Loss, Trump Could Face New Challenges on Tariffs
articles.abstract: Critics are questioning the legality of the provision President Trump has used to replace his previous slate of tariffs, raising the prospect of yet another legal battle.

articles.title: Here Are the Countries and Products Subject to Tariffs Now
articles.abstract: President Trump ramped up his tariff campaign, one day after the Supreme Court invalidated a large chunk of duties he had previously imposed on most of the world’s imports.

articles.abstract: The tariff ruling may have just helped save the Republic.

articles.title: Congress Faces War Powers Votes in Wake of Iran Strikes
articles.abstract: Following the attack, Democrats and a few Republicans escalated their calls for swift votes on whether to curb the president’s power to continue using force against Iran without explicit authorization.

articles.title: Justice Gorsuch’s Tariffs Warning
articles.abstract: Granting a president new power is easy, he said. But taking it back is almost impossible.
articles.snippet: Granting a president new power is easy, he said. But taking it back is almost impossible.
